page.content: Nestlé SA's 5-Year CAGR report for the Food & Beverage sector reveals essential metrics across key performance indicators. For Units Sold, the company recorded 25M, while Revenue Growth stood at 5.2%. The Headcount was noted at 3,500, and the Number of Branches totaled 150, with an Average Price of CHF4.50. In examining sustainability metrics, the Packaging Waste for the year 2019 was 150,000 tons, with a Recycling Rate of 35%.

lines.text: Nestlé SA's 5-Year CAGR report for the Food & Beverage sector reveals essential metrics across key performance indicators.
For Units Sold, the company recorded 25M, while Revenue Growth stood at 5.2%. The Headcount was noted at 3,500, and the
Number of Branches totaled 150, with an Average Price of CHF4.50. In examining sustainability metrics, the Packaging Waste
for the year 2019 was 150,000 tons, with a Recycling Rate of 35%.

page.content: The Total Volume of Plastic Reduction in 2020 was 145,000 tons, with a Recycling Rate of 38%. In 2021, the Total Volume decreased to 140,000 tons, while the Recycling Rate improved to 40%. The year 2022 saw a further decline in Total Volume to 135,000 tons, with a Recycling Rate of 45%. By 2023, the Total Volume reached 130,000 tons, reflecting a Recycling Rate of 50%. The 5-Year CAGR for Packaging Waste was -3.2%, while Plastic Reduction achieved a CAGR of 4.5%, Fiber Use at 5.5%, CO2 Emissions at -2.3%, Water Usage at -5.0%, Energy Consumption at 2.5%, and Waste Diversion at 3.0%.

decreased to 140,000 tons, while the Recycling Rate improved to 40%. The year 2022 saw a further decline in Total Volume to

50%. The 5-Year CAGR for Packaging Waste was -3.2%, while Plastic Reduction achieved a CAGR of 4.5%, Fiber Use at
5.5%, CO2 Emissions at -2.3%, Water Usage at -5.0%, Energy Consumption at 2.5%, and Waste Diversion at 3.0%.

page.content: Sustainable materials increased from 60% in 2019 to 70% in 2023, highlighting Nestlé’s commitment to sustainability throughout the reporting period.
